title: Proclamation No. 267, s. 1967
text:
MALACAÑANG RESIDENCE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 267

DECLARING WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 27, 1967, AS A SPECIAL PUBLIC HOLIDAY IN THE PROVINCE OF BATANGAS AND LIPA CITY.

WHEREAS, General Miguel Malvar is one of the most heroic figures in our history, being the last Filipino General to surrender in the Filipino-American War; and

WHEREAS, the people of Batangas should be given full opportunity to celebrate the 102nd birthday of General Malvar on September 27, 1967;

NOW, THEREFORE, I, FERDINAND E. MARCOS, President of the Philippines, pursuant to the authority vested in me by Section 30 of the Revised Administrative Code, do hereby declare Wednesday, September 27, 1967, as a special public holiday in the province of Batangas and Lipa City.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 22nd day of September, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and sixty-seven.

(SGD.) FERDINAND E. MARCOS President of the Philippines

By the President: (SGD.) RAFAEL M. SALAS Executive Secretary

Source : CDAsia
